howItWorks.intro: This wood stain calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Coverage = Surface Area × Number of Coats × (1 + Waste %)
Quarts Needed = Total Coverage ÷ Coverage per Quart
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much area does a quart of stain cover?
faq.answer: Oil-based stain: 200-300 sq ft/qt. Water-based stain: 250-350 sq ft/qt. Polyurethane: 300-400 sq ft/qt. Rough or porous wood absorbs more and reduces coverage.

faq.question: How many coats of stain do I need?
faq.answer: Most stains need 1-2 coats. Polyurethane needs 2-3 coats with light sanding between. Deck stain typically needs 2 coats. Apply thin, even coats rather than one thick coat.

faq.question: Should I buy quarts or gallons?
faq.answer: Gallons are typically 20-30% cheaper per ounce than quarts. If you need more than 3 quarts, a gallon is usually more economical. Leftover stain stores well for touch-ups.
